title: Stacks, queues and optional values

canonicalExplanation.summary: Container adaptors expose a restricted stack, queue, or priority-queue interface over an underlying container to enforce access discipline.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A bracket checker uses stack top and pop only after verifying the stack is nonempty. Your goal is to implement a small workflow with stack, queue or optional and test empty-state behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Loops and range iteration; Copy and assignment semantics; string, array and vector. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Container adaptors expose a restricted stack, queue, or priority-queue interface over an underlying container to enforce access discipline. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Adaptors do not expose ordinary iteration, priority_queue order depends on its comparator, and front, top, or pop require nonempty preconditions. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: Calling pop on a standard stack returns the removed value like some other languages' stack APIs.

includes: Use container adaptors and explicit absence rather than sentinel misuse.
Implement a small workflow with stack, queue or optional and test empty-state behavior.
excludes: Adaptors do not expose ordinary iteration, priority_queue order depends on its comparator, and front, top, or pop require nonempty preconditions.

outcomes: Implement a small workflow with stack, queue or optional and test empty-state behavior.

examples.title: Stacks, queues and optional values: defined case
examples.situation: A bracket checker uses stack top and pop only after verifying the stack is nonempty.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A bracket checker uses stack top and pop only after verifying the stack is nonempty.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Standard container-adaptor pop removes without returning; read top or front first after checking nonempty, then call pop.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Standard container-adaptor pop removes without returning; read top or front first after checking nonempty, then call pop.

examples.title: Stacks, queues and optional values: boundary case
examples.situation: A task scheduler defines a comparator so priority_queue top returns the intended highest-priority task rather than assuming pair ordering.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A task scheduler defines a comparator so priority_queue top returns the intended highest-priority task rather than assuming pair ordering.
Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Adaptors do not expose ordinary iteration, priority_queue order depends on its comparator, and front, top, or pop require nonempty preconditions.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: Calling pop on a standard stack returns the removed value like some other languages' stack APIs.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement a small workflow with stack, queue or optional and test empty-state behavior.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: Calling pop on a standard stack returns the removed value like some other languages' stack APIs.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Stacks, queues and optional values: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
